title: satureja ericifolia subsp. meridialis
intro: Satureja ericifolia subsp. meridialis, also known as Spanish savory, is a subspecies of the Satureja ericifolia plant, a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to its essential oil composition. The health benefits of Spanish savory include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Spanish savory can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infused oils, which are often used to support respiratory health and relieve muscle tension.
